inputs.prompt: Title: The Repair Shop Floor. Genre: Documentary series. Subject: A mobile electronics repair cooperative that travels to rural towns offering free device repair clinics — each episode documents one town's relationship with technology, waste, and community self-sufficiency. Episodes: 6 x 30 minutes. Target audience: 25-45, environmentally conscious, mainstream documentary viewers. Target broadcaster: BBC Three.
Title: Night Shift. Genre: Observational documentary. Subject: Life inside a 24-hour hospital emergency department. Episodes: 4 x 52 min. Audience: General.

Title: The Danube Diaries. Genre: Documentary series. Subject: Five families living along the Danube from Germany to Romania, each representing a different national relationship with the river — industry, ecology, tourism, agriculture, displacement. Episodes: 5 x 52 min. Target: Arte. Comparables: Nile Hilton Incident (tone), Human (scale).

Title: The Bone Setters. Genre: Documentary series. Subject: Traditional bone-setting practitioners across rural India who treat fractures and dislocations without formal medical training — each episode follows one practitioner and the community that relies on them as the nearest hospital is hours away. The series explores faith, risk, medical access, and the tension between traditional knowledge and modern regulation. Episodes: 4 x 45 min. Target audience: 30-55, international documentary viewers. Target broadcaster: Al Jazeera English. Comparables: The Waiting Room (intimacy), The Surgeon's Cut (medical stakes).

Title: Everything Everywhere. Genre: Documentary series. Subject: It's about life. Modern life and how everything is connected — technology, nature, relationships, money, food, politics, art. Each episode explores a different theme but they all link together in surprising ways. Episodes: Maybe 8 or 10, length flexible. Audience: Everyone. Target: Netflix or whoever.

inputs.description: African context - East African wildlife conservation
inputs.prompt: Title: The Last Corridor. Genre: Documentary series. Subject: The wildlife corridors connecting national parks in Kenya and Tanzania are being severed by roads, fences, and settlements. Each episode follows a different corridor and the rangers, pastoralist communities, and conservationists trying to keep migration routes open. The series examines what happens when an elephant herd's ancient path meets a new highway. Episodes: 6 x 52 min. Target: BBC Two. Target audience: 35-60, natural history and current affairs crossover. Comparables: The Ivory Game (urgency), Planet Earth (production value), The Mara (local perspective).

inputs.description: Stress test - extremely ambitious multi-platform pitch
inputs.prompt: Title: The Water Table. Genre: Multi-platform documentary franchise. Subject: A global investigation into who owns freshwater — from aquifer rights in the American Midwest to desalination monopolies in the Gulf States, glacier-fed agriculture in the Andes, and dam politics on the Mekong. The project spans four continents and includes a 6 x 52-minute flagship series (target: HBO), a companion 10-episode podcast featuring hydrologists and water activists, an interactive web documentary mapping every privatised water source globally, a theatrical feature cut for festival circuit, and a 20-part short-form social series for Instagram and TikTok. Total budget estimate: $4.2 million. Timeline: 24 months from greenlight. The creative team includes two directors, a data journalist, and a podcast producer. Comparables: An Inconvenient Truth (impact), The Social Dilemma (multi-format reach), Don't Look Up (cultural urgency).
